headlines.title: Q1 Rundown: Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA) Vs Other Consumer Internet Stocks
headlines.summary: As the craze of earnings season draws to a close, here’s a look back at some of the most exciting (and some less so) results from Q1. Today, we are looking at consumer internet stocks, starting with Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA).

headlines.title: EA SPORTS™ College Football 27 Launches Worldwide, Bringing New Era of College Football to Life
headlines.summary: REDWOOD CITY, Calif., July 09, 2026--Electronic Arts Inc. (NASDAQ: EA) today launched EA SPORTS™ College Football 27 worldwide on PlayStation®5, Xbox Series X|S and, for the first time ever, PC and mobile. Following the record-breaking return of the franchise, College Football 27 raises the bar once again, delivering the most authentic college football experience yet.

headlines.title: 3 Inflated  Stocks We Steer Clear Of
headlines.summary: The stocks featured in this article have all approached their 52-week highs. When these price levels hit, it typically signals strong business execution, positive market sentiment, or significant industry tailwinds.

headlines.title: Should You Buy Electronic Arts Inc. (EA)’s Shares?
headlines.summary: Electronic Arts Inc. (NASDAQ:EA) is one of the 10 Best Stocks to Buy According to Billionaire Glenn Dubin’s Highbridge Capital. Video game developer Electronic Arts Inc. (NASDAQ:EA)’s shares are up by 28% over the past year and are flat year-to-date.

headlines.title: Fitch Withdraws EA Bond Rating Amid $55 Billion Buyout Dispute
headlines.summary: Bondholders argue the rating withdrawal could strengthen their claim for a change-of-control premium.

description: Electronic Arts Inc. develops, markets, publishes, and delivers games, content, and services for game consoles, PCs, and mobile phones worldwide. It develops and publishes games and experiences across diverse genres, such as sports, racing, first-person shooter, action, role-playing, and simulation; and live services offerings, including extra content and subscription offerings through its global football and American football franchises, such as EA SPORTS College Football and EA SPORTS Madden NFL, as well as based on its IP comprising The Sims, Apex Legends, and Battlefield. The company markets and sells its games and services through digital distribution and retail channels; and directly to mass market retailers, specialty stores, and distribution arrangements. Electronic Arts Inc. was incorporated in 1982 and is headquartered in Redwood City, California.
